text: The noexec mount option prevents execution of any binaries on the mounted filesystem. Use it to harden /tmp and /var so that uploaded programs cannot be run directly from those paths.

The nosuid mount option causes the set-user-identifier and set-group-identifier bits to be ignored on the mounted filesystem. This blocks privilege escalation through suid binaries placed on removable or untrusted media.

The ro mount option mounts the filesystem read-only. No writes are permitted until it is remounted read-write with mount -o remount,rw. This protects a recovery or forensic image from accidental modification.

firewall-cmd --panic-on drops all incoming and outgoing packets, severing every network connection including the active SSH session. Use firewall-cmd --panic-off to restore traffic. Panic mode is a destructive lockout operation on a remote host.

A firewalld zone defines the trust level of a network connection. The public zone rejects most incoming traffic while the trusted zone accepts everything. Assign an interface to a zone with firewall-cmd --zone=public --change-interface=eth0.
systemctl mask links a unit to /dev/null so it can never be started, even as a dependency of another unit. This is stronger than disable. Unmask it with systemctl unmask before it can run again.
dnf history undo <id> reverses a previous transaction, reinstalling removed packages and removing installed ones. Run dnf history list to find the transaction id of the change you want to roll back.
lvextend -r -L +10G /dev/vg0/data grows a logical volume by ten gigabytes and, with -r, resizes the filesystem on it in the same step. Shrinking instead requires unmounting and is risky for ext4.

When SELinux blocks an operation it logs an AVC denial. Run ausearch -m avc -ts recent to view recent denials and audit2allow to generate a candidate policy module. A dontaudit rule suppresses noisy denials from the log.
journalctl --since '1 hour ago' -u nginx.service shows log entries for the nginx unit within the last hour. Combine with -p err to filter by priority and -f to follow new entries as they arrive.
mkfs.ext4 /dev/sdb1 creates a new ext4 filesystem and irreversibly destroys all existing data on the target device. There is no undo. Verify the device path against lsblk before formatting.

ServerAliveInterval 60 in ssh_config sends a keepalive packet every sixty seconds so an idle SSH session is not dropped by a stateful firewall. ServerAliveCountMax sets how many missed replies end the connection.
